pages.title: Agent Handoff Patterns: Routing Work Between AI Agents

pages.title: Agent2Agent Protocol — A2A Security, Architecture, and MCP Comparison
pages.description: Agent2Agent (A2A) protocol lets AI agents delegate tasks across frameworks and vendors. Learn the architecture, security model, task lifecycle, and how A2A compares to MCP for multi-agent systems.

pages.title: Agentic RAG: Sub-Question Decomposition, HyDE, and Corpus Security
pages.description: How agentic RAG works: iterative retrieval loops, sub-question decomposition, HyDE, FLARE, and re-ranking. Covers multi-agent pipelines, corpus injection attacks, and embedding model selection.

pages.title: Agentic Workflow - Patterns, Security, and Production Design
pages.description: An agentic workflow is a multi-step AI process where agents autonomously choose tools, delegate subtasks, and adapt based on intermediate results. Core patterns, failure modes, and security design.

pages.title: AI Agent Architecture — Layers, Topologies, and Security-First Design
pages.description: AI agent architecture: four structural layers (perception, reasoning, memory, action), single vs multi-agent topologies, ReAct loop, and 2025 cross-agent standards (Google A2A, OpenAI handoffs).

pages.title: AI Agent Benchmarks: SWE-bench, GAIA, WebArena, and Private Evals
pages.description: AI agent benchmarks: SWE-bench Verified scores, GAIA levels, AgentBench environments, tau-bench policy compliance, and WebArena results, plus how to run private evals on your own task distribution.

pages.title: AI Agent Context Window — Management, Limits, and Security
pages.description: AI agent context window management: token budgets, context poisoning via OWASP LLM01:2025, compression strategies, and how OpenLegion enforces per-agent context isolation.

pages.title: AI Agent Deployment: Container TTLs, Budget Caps, and Circuit Breakers
pages.description: Deploy AI agents to production: container TTLs, per-agent budget caps, LLM circuit breakers, credential vault proxy, graceful shutdown, and Kubernetes HPA for agent fleets.

pages.title: AI Agent Evaluation: Benchmarks, Metrics, and Testing
pages.description: AI agent evaluation is the discipline of measuring whether agents reliably complete tasks, use tools safely, and stay within cost bounds — covering benchmarks, LLM-as-judge, and trace analysis.

pages.title: Best AI Agent Frameworks (2026 Comparison)

pages.title: AI Agent MCP Security: Red-Team Guide to Exploit Prevention
pages.description: Red-team guide to AI agent MCP security: tool poisoning exploits, rug-pull backdoors, supply chain attacks, cross-pipeline injection, and infrastructure hardening.

pages.title: AI Agent Memory: Four Types, Security Risks, and Implementation
pages.description: AI agent memory: in-context, vector store, structured K-V, and episodic types. Security risks from memory poisoning and credential exposure. OpenLegion vault-protected architecture.

pages.title: AI Agent Monitoring — Thresholds, Liveness, and Escalation
pages.description: AI agent monitoring: SLO definition, runaway loop detection, heartbeat liveness checks, budget enforcement cutoffs, and escalation path design for production agent fleets.

pages.description: Container-isolated multi-agent runtime with fleet-model coordination (blackboard + pub/sub + handoff), credential vaulting, and per-agent budget controls.

pages.title: AI Agent Platform — Deploy Secure Agents
pages.description: OpenLegion is a managed AI agent platform with container isolation, credential vaulting, and budget controls. Bring your own LLM API keys.

pages.title: AI Agent Prompt Injection: Attack Types, Defences, and Red-Teaming
pages.description: How prompt injection targets AI agents: direct, indirect, stored, and multi-agent relay attacks. Defence via instruction hierarchy, spotlighting, input sanitisation, and output validation.

pages.title: AI Agent Sandboxing — Container Isolation, Escape CVEs, and Hardening
pages.description: How AI agent sandboxing works: Docker, gVisor, WebAssembly, and seccomp hardening. Covers sandbox escape CVEs, code execution isolation, browser agent sandboxing, and runtime cost trade-offs.

pages.title: AI Agent Security — Threats, Isolation, Vaults
pages.description: AI agent security guide: credential leakage, prompt injection, sandbox escape, and how container isolation, vault-proxied credentials, and budget controls mitigate each threat.

pages.title: AI Agent State Management — Checkpointing, Shared State, and Crash Recovery
pages.description: Checkpointing, shared state, and crash recovery for AI agents. Covers LangGraph checkpointers, transactional blackboard writes, reducer patterns, snapshot isolation, and cross-agent synchronization.

pages.title: AI Agent Tool Use — Function Calling, Schemas, and Safe Execution
pages.description: How AI agents use tools: defining tool schemas, function calling across providers, parallel and chained calls, output parsing, error recovery, and permission scoping for safe production execution.

pages.title: AI Coding Agents - Deploy Secure Dev Agent Teams
pages.description: AI coding agents that plan, write, test, and review code autonomously. OpenLegion runs them in isolated containers with vaulted credentials and per-agent budgets - a self-hostable dev agent team.

pages.title: Browser Use Agents — How AI Agents Control the Web
pages.description: Browser use agents let AI autonomously navigate websites, fill forms, and extract data. Learn how they work, their security risks, and how to run them safely in isolated containers.

pages.description: Claude Opus 4.8 launched May 28 2026: 84% on Online-Mind2Web, fast mode 3x cheaper, dynamic workflows in Claude Code, first Opus to complete every Super-Agent benchmark case at GPT-5.5 cost parity.

pages.title: Credential Management for AI Agents: Vault-Proxy Architecture
pages.description: Vault-proxy architecture for AI agents: inject secrets server-side, isolate per agent, audit every access, and avoid the env-var anti-pattern that exposed keys in CVE-2024-34359 and CVE-2025-29927.

pages.description: Grok 4 is xAI's most capable reasoning model, released July 9 2025, trained on a 200,000-GPU cluster with RL at pretraining scale, native tool use, and API access via grok-4 model string.

pages.title: Multi-Agent Systems Architecture - Design, Topology, and Security
pages.description: Multi-agent systems architecture defines how autonomous agents communicate, coordinate, and maintain trust boundaries. Covers topologies, inter-agent protocols, and production security design.

pages.description: What is an AI agent? A clear definition: an autonomous system that perceives, plans, and acts toward a goal using tools - plus how agents differ from chatbots and how the agent loop works.
